name: Sliced Chicken Breast on Caponata
recipeIngredient: 2 teaspoons Olive oil, preferably extra virgin
2 small Onions (3 ounces each), halved and thinly sliced lengthwise
1 cup Diced bell peppers, combination of yellow and red
3/4 cup Balsamic or red wine vinegar
1/4 teaspoon Freshly ground pepper to taste
1 Whole, boneless, skinless Chicken breast, split
1 teaspoon Fresh thyme leaves or 1 pinch Dried thyme
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Add onions and garlic; cook, stirring occasionally, until onions are softened, about 3 minutes. Stir in eggplant, bell peppers, zucchini, chicken broth, and 3/4 cup vinegar. Cook, stirring occasionally, over medium heat until softened, about 5 minutes. Stir in tomatoes, currants, capers, and 1/4 teaspoon each salt and pepper; cook about 5 minutes longer. Set caponata aside.
2. Heat vegetable oil in a medium non-stick skillet over medium heat. Sprinkle chicken lightly with salt, then coat lightly with flour. Add to oil and cook, turning once, just until cooked through, 4-5 minutes each side.
3. Stir remaining 1 teaspoon vinegar and thyme into caponata and gently heat just until hot.
4. Slice chicken diagonally into 3/4-inch thick strips. Spoon caponata onto serving plates and top with chicken strips. Serve hot.
